top_stories.title: Idira launches as Palo Alto Networks extends CyberArk tech to machine and agentic identities
top_stories.summary: Palo Alto Networks launched Idira, a new identity security platform that manages human, machine, and AI agent identities under a single PAM framework. The platform incorporates CyberArk technology to extend privileged access management capabilities to machine and agentic identities.

top_stories.title: Hackers Used AI to Develop First Known Zero-Day 2FA Bypass for Mass Exploitation
top_stories.summary: Google disclosed that an unknown threat actor used a zero-day exploit likely developed with AI to bypass two-factor authentication (2FA), marking the first known instance of AI being used in the wild for malicious vulnerability discovery and exploit generation. The exploit was attributed to cybercrime threat actors and represents a novel attack vector directly targeting 2FA mechanisms used widely in identity and access management.

top_stories.title: Palo Alto Networks Introduces Idira: The Next-Generation Identity Security Platform Built for the AI Enterprise
top_stories.summary: Palo Alto Networks has introduced Idira, a next-generation identity security platform designed for AI-driven enterprises. The platform appears to target identity security challenges specific to AI environments, representing a new entrant in the IAM/identity security market from a major cybersecurity vendor.

top_stories.title: New Semperis Study Reveals AI's Effects on the Identity Attack Surface
top_stories.summary: Semperis released a study examining how AI agents are expanding the identity attack surface, particularly around Active Directory, Entra ID, and Okta. The research found organizations are granting AI agents access to critical systems before implementing adequate identity security safeguards.

top_stories.title: Keeper Security Delivers Structured Governance for Humans and AI Agents with New PAM Approval Workflows in KeeperPAM
top_stories.summary: Keeper Security has launched new PAM Approval Workflows within its KeeperPAM platform, introducing structured governance and approval-based controls for privileged access. The new Keeper Workflow capability is designed to provide administrators with oversight of privileged access for both human users and AI agents across their organizations.

news.title: Zombie linkages are keeping expired domains trusted for years
news.summary: Researchers from USC and the University of Twente identified "zombie linkages" in Web PKI, Maven Central, and Ethereum Name Service, where trust records persist after domain ownership changes. Expired domains that are re-registered by new owners can inherit trust relationships originally established by previous owners, creating security vulnerabilities in certificate-based authentication systems.

news.title: CISA Adds Cisco SD-WAN CVE-2026-20182 to KEV After Admin Access Exploits
news.summary: CISA added CVE-2026-20182, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Federal Civilian Executive Branch agencies are required to remediate the issue by May 17, 2026.

news.title: HYCU  aiR detects insider risk and AI activity from backups
news.summary: HYCU announced HYCU aiR (AI Resilience), an AI-native solution that analyzes backup data across multiple applications to detect insider risk, sensitive data exposure, identity drift, and AI agent activity. The solution treats backups as timestamped records of organizational activity, enabling security and compliance teams to query and run agents against that historical data.

news.title: Microsoft turns Copilot Studio into an AI agent control center
news.summary: Microsoft released April 2026 updates to Copilot Studio that add security and governance features for AI agents, including visibility into agent security posture and authentication gaps. The update also introduces a generally available Analytics Viewer role that controls access to analytics insights within the platform.

news.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller Auth Bypass Actively Exploited to Gain Admin Access
news.summary: Cisco has released patches for a maximum-severity authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-20182, CVSS 10.0) in Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and SD-WAN Manager that is being actively exploited. The flaw affects the peering authentication mechanism, allowing attackers to gain administrative access without valid credentials.

news.title: Microsoft Patches 138 Vulnerabilities, Including DNS and Netlogon RCE Flaws
news.summary: Microsoft released patches for 138 security vulnerabilities, including 30 rated Critical, covering flaws in DNS and Netlogon among other products. 61 of the vulnerabilities are privilege escalation bugs, which are directly relevant to access control and identity security.

news.title: AI threats push Middle East CISOs towards identity-first security
news.summary: Middle East CISOs are shifting toward identity-first security strategies in response to AI-driven threats such as deepfakes and shadow AI. The move reflects a broader trend of prioritizing identity as the central security control as traditional perimeter-based defenses become less effective.

news.title: Analyzing TeamPCP’s Supply Chain Attacks: Checkmarx KICS and elementary-data in CI/CD Credential Theft
news.summary: Researchers analyzed two supply chain attacks (April 22 and April 24) attributed to a threat actor called TeamPCP, targeting Checkmarx KICS and elementary-data. The campaign abused CI/CD and release workflows to steal credentials at scale across both incidents.

news.title: Fortinet warns of critical RCE flaws in FortiSandbox and FortiAuthenticator
news.summary: Fortinet has released security patches for two critical vulnerabilities affecting FortiSandbox and FortiAuthenticator that could allow attackers to execute commands or arbitrary code. FortiAuthenticator is Fortinet's authentication and identity management product, making these flaws directly relevant to IAM infrastructure security.

news.title: Two-Thirds of Enterprises Suspect AI Agents Have Already Accessed Unauthorized Data, Akeyless Finds
news.summary: A global study by Akeyless found that two-thirds of enterprises suspect AI agents have already accessed unauthorized data, with most organizations unable to detect compromised AI agents for hours. The majority of respondents reported spending over $1 million managing the fallout from AI agent security incidents, highlighting gaps in authorization and access controls for non-human identities.

news.title: Saviynt Strengthens Executive Team to Accelerate AI Identity Security Leadership
news.summary: Saviynt has appointed Pete Angstadt as its first Chief Commercial Officer and Brad Myers as Chief Customer Officer. Both are described as industry veterans joining the IAM/IGA vendor's executive team.

news.title: Are You Using the Right Security Controls for Agent 365?
news.summary: Microsoft's Agent 365 platform integrates with Microsoft Entra as part of its security and compliance framework for managing AI agents. The platform combines identity and access controls through Microsoft Entra alongside Microsoft Purview and Microsoft Defender to enforce security standards for AI agent management.

news.title: Descope Named to Rising in Cyber 2026 List of Top Cybersecurity Startups
news.summary: Descope, a customer identity and authentication platform, has been named to the Rising in Cyber 2026 list of top cybersecurity startups, as voted by CISOs. The list recognizes 30 private cybersecurity companies, with Descope included for its work in identity and authentication.

news.title: Attacks Abuse Windows Phone Link to Steal Texts & Bypass 2FA
news.summary: Attackers are abusing Windows Phone Link functionality to steal SMS messages and bypass two-factor authentication using a remote access trojan called CloudZ and a new plugin named Pheno. The attack hijacks the Windows-based bridge between PCs and smartphones to intercept SMS-based 2FA codes, effectively undermining a common authentication control.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a senior member of the cybercrime group Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft for his role in SMS phishing attacks in 2022. The attacks targeted at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in the theft of tens of millions of dollars in cryptocurrency through credential theft and social engineering techniques.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign operated without deploying malware, relying solely on token theft to silently compromise user access.
